title: quercus infectoria subsp. mirbeckii
intro: Quercus infectoria subsp. mirbeckii, also known as Gall Oak or Oak Gall, is a plant species that belongs to the Fagaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus infectoria subsp. mirbeckii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Traditionally, herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures are used to harness the plant's medicinal properties, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
